Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=UNKNOWN}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|くとしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

Culture ID (P9475) == * E0005697 [[JA:久度神社 (王寺町)]] {{wikidata link|Q115748481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kudo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kudo-jinja (Oji), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}} Kudo 4-9-1 |緯度度=34|緯度分=35|緯度秒=57|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=135|経度分=41|経度秒=52|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |祭神=Kudo Ōkami / Hachiman Ōkami / Sumiyoshi Ōkami / Kasuga Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Kudo Shrine''' (くどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōji|Q1346392|WD=Q1346392|1=Ōji, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Kitakatsuragi District|Q1131013|WD=Q1131013|1=Kitakatsuragi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#shosha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}, it is written as “Kudono Shrine” (クトノジンジャ). === Overview === The ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' says that in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 2 (783), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} and granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. It is also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Kudo no Kami, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}.” Also, at the second seat of {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}} (former {{ill|lt=kanpei taisha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) in Kita Ward, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, founded at the time of the {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō relocation|Q1389395|WD=Q1389395|1=Juan Bautista Massa}} in Enryaku 13 (794), Kudo-no-Kami was enshrined after being transferred from this shrine{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kamnavi.jp/as/ikoma/kudo.htm |title=[Kannabi] Kudo Shrine |access-date=2022-12-17}}. The enshrined deities are Kudo Ōkami, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}Shrine notice board. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ''{{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}'', “Kudo-no-Kami” was not recorded, but in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43, Kudo-no-Kami was invited from Hirano Shrine (former kanpei taisha) and enshrined in the main sanctuary of Kudo Shrine, the original seat. Kudo Ōkami is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kitchen hearth deity|1=Kitchen God|WD=Q249178}}{{Cite web|title=Hirano Shrine|url=https://www.hiranojinja.com/home/gaiyo|access-date=2025-10-26}}. Kudo Ōkami was conferred Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eihō|Q1193881|WD=Q1193881|1=Eihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1081|Q19450|WD=Q19450|1=1081}}){{Cite web|title=Nara Prefecture Jinja-chō: Kudo Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-nara/jsearch3nara.php?jinjya=5810|access-date=2025-10-29}}. The oldest stone lantern at the shrine is from {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 1 (1338) in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, and a {{ill|lt=Kasuga Mandala|Q11513629|WD=Q11513629|1=Kasuga Mandalas}} with a box inscription from {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 8 (1476) is deposited at {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.oji.nara.jp/kakuka/chiikiseibi/chiikikoryu/ojicyorekishishiryoshitsu/ojibunkazai/148.html |title=Kudo Shrine / Ōji Town |access-date=2022-12-22 |publisher=Ōji Town}}. === Access === About 10 minutes on foot west of the north side of {{ill|lt=Ōji Station|Q856582|WD=Q856582|1=Ōji Station (Nara)}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Takano no Niigasa|Q7677188|WD=Q7677188|1=Takano no Niigasa}} === External links === * {{Instagram|kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} * {{Facebook|Kudoshrine|Kudo Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kudo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:王寺町の建築物]] [[Category:王寺町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]
